title: Language Translation - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Language Translation 9 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF AT:PROJECT ANTI-TRAFFICKING ALLIANCE OF CENTRAL OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Anti-Trafficking Project and Cannabis Workers Program provides human and labor trafficking hotline services, including safety planning, risk assessments, hygiene items, clothing, food, mental health, substance abuse treatment referrals, applying for public assistance benefits, and document replacement. In addition, the program offers assistance with GED, resume building, transportation to interviews, and referrals to local services for job training and career development. Case management and medical accompaniment services are also available. All services are confidential. Eligibility: Anti-Trafficking Project: Individuals who are experiencing or survivors of sex or labor trafficking or exploitation Youth who have experience Commercial Sexual Exploitation of Children (CSEC). Cannabis Workers Program: Individuals who have worked in the marijuana industry who have experienced wage loss, unsafe living and working conditions, violence, labor exploitation, or human trafficking. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 647-0658 Visit Website More Details CASA LATINOS UNIDOS FAMILY EMPOWERMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides wraparound support, transportation, language interpretation, assistance filling out forms (e.g., W-2s, job applications, rental forms, and appointments with other agencies), navigation and enrollment for the Oregon Health Plan, and assistance with documentation of discrimination. 3460 Midvale Drive Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Members of the Latinx community Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 512-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASA LATINOS UNIDOS LEBANON OFFICE FAMILY EMPOWERMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides wraparound support, transportation, language interpretation, assistance filling out forms (e.g., W-2s, job applications, rental forms, and appointments with other agencies), navigation and enrollment for the Oregon Health Plan, and assistance with documentation of discrimination. 500 Weldwood Drive Building E Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Members of the Latinx community Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 512-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRO DE AYUDA WRITTEN TRANSLATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides written translation services for Spanish-speaking clients. Types of documents that can be translated include but are not limited to medical records, court documents, informative handouts, letters, and emails. 410 SW 9th Street Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Spanish-speaking individuals Hours: Monday-Friday 1pm-7pm Email (541) 265-6216 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO INFORMATION AND REFERRAL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referral to community resources for community members requiring assistance with accessing resources. 333 SE 223rd Avenue Suite 100 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Community members who identify as Hispanic or Latinx Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm Email (503) 669-8350 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION INTERNATIONAL LANGUAGE BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides interpretation and translation services. Also provides community center rentals and diversity training. 10301 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 271-6481 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER COLONIA AMISTAD FAMILY WELLNESS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps people access community resources such as food, health care, and civil rights education. Also provides advocacy, case management, and referrals to local partners. Assists with preparing documents and completing forms for public benefits, legal issues, passports, DMV, medical, rental, and job applications. Services include Power of Attorney, travel consent for minors, translations, and notarization. 30 S Gun Club Road Independence, OR 97351 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, or by appointment Email (503) 917-8024 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER COLONIA LIBERTAD FAMILY WELLNESS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps people access community resources such as food, health care, and civil rights education. Also provides advocacy, case management, and referrals to local partners. Assists with preparing documents and completing forms for public benefits, legal issues, passports, DMV, medical, rental, and job applications. Services include Power of Attorney, travel consent for minors, translations, and notarization. 2921 Saddle Club Street SE #1009 Salem, OR 97317 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 315-2290 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER NORTHGATE CENTER FAMILY WELLNESS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps people access community resources such as food, health care, and civil rights education.
